news.title: Hong Kong Slides in Global English Rankings
news.newsText: In November 2025, the EF English Proficiency Index revealed Hong Kong plummeted to 39th globally with a score of 538, marking a fourth consecutive year of decline. The city now trails Malaysia and the Philippines in Asia. Experts ascribe the alarming trajectory to pandemic-era disruptions in interactive learning, with youth proficiency particularly sluggish despite post-pandemic recovery efforts.
news.reflectionText: Hong Kong scored 550 in Reading versus a dismal 494 in Speaking. This lays bare the local education system's overemphasis on exam-oriented reading and writing at the expense of conversational fluency. The sharp decline among 18–20-year-olds highlights the unintended consequences of prolonged online learning, where muted video lessons and masked classrooms eliminated opportunities for spoken practice. The decline calls for a pedagogical shift away from rote memorization and exam drills toward interactive, speaking-focused instruction. Reasons include: diminishing exposure to English outside classrooms / over-reliance on rote learning / reduced opportunities for immersive interactions with native speakers / proliferation of short-form social media content in Chinese / students' reluctance to speak English for fear of losing face / phasing out of NETs due to budget constraints.

news.title: HKUST's New Medical School
news.newsText: In November 2025, the government approved HKUST's proposal to establish the city's third medical school. Leveraging its strengths in artificial intelligence and biomedical technology, HKUST aims to nurture tech-savvy doctors through an interdisciplinary curriculum. The school has forged partnerships with around 40 renowned medical institutions globally.
news.reflectionText: The establishment of Hong Kong's third medical school would address critical doctor shortages driven by an aging population, alleviating the strain on the city's overstretched healthcare system. Expanding medical training capacity may reduce chronic understaffing in public hospitals, potentially reducing medical blunders and burnout. HKUST's school pioneers a paradigm shift in medical education by integrating cutting-edge technologies, equipping graduates with competencies for precision medicine and data-driven clinical decision-making. The school's partnerships demonstrate Hong Kong's positioning as a Greater Bay Area medical hub bridging Chinese and Western healthcare. AI case studies: NHS breast cancer mammography trial (700,000 women, Feb 2025) / AI stroke detection / 'Sybil' deep-learning model for lung cancer risk.

news.title: Hong Kong's Worst Fire in Decades
news.newsText: On November 26, 2025, a catastrophic blaze erupted at Wang Fuk Court, a Tai Po housing estate undergoing renovations, claiming at least 160 lives. Flames rapidly engulfed seven residential towers covered in bamboo scaffolding and mesh netting, with authorities attributing the swift spread to substandard flammable materials, including styrofoam boards.
news.reflectionText: The tragedy has exposed critical failures in building safety oversight. It highlighted dangers of cost-cutting in construction, as investigators probe suspected corruption in the tendering process. The tragedy lays bare a bitter irony: in one of Asia's wealthiest cities, highly combustible materials and non-functional fire alarms caused the deadliest residential high-rise fire in the 21st century, exposing how prosperity could mask deep-rooted regulatory failures. The outpouring of support — from McDonald's providing free meals to HK$3.7 billion in public donations — reinforces Hong Kong's reputation as a city of compassion. Volunteers' efforts to rescue residents' pets reflect evolving attitudes toward animal welfare.

news.title: Travel KOL Acquires Record-Breaking Luxury Unit
news.newsText: In November 2025, 90s-born travel KOL 'FlyForMiles' (張鎵安 Cheung Lim-on), renowned for teaching credit card rewards strategies, splurged HK$136.8 million on a North Point Harbour Grand II penthouse unit. The 2,118-sq-ft 'phoenix floor' unit with rooftop terrace fetched HK$64,589 per square foot, shattering Harbour Grand II's price records.
news.reflectionText: This record-breaking transaction has drawn attention to how influencer marketing has evolved into a formidable revenue stream, with industry leaders earning upwards of HK$50 million annually through diversified partnerships. Niche expertise can be parlayed into substantial wealth in the digital economy. The case prompts reflection on the widening wealth gap within the creator economy, where a handful of industry leaders command eight-figure incomes while smaller influencers struggle to sustain basic livelihoods. Conspicuous consumption might inadvertently inspire younger generations to pursue influencer careers for financial gain rather than genuine passion, saturating the market with mercenary creators.

phrases.phrase: Among all the + N., the most noteworthy one is that ...
phrases.zh: 在眾多…中，最值得注意的是…

example.sentence: Among all the arguments against the removal of PE lessons, the most noteworthy one is that it will put students' health at great risk.

notes.text: TS (Topic Sentence) — for = 辯駁對手

phrases.zh: 沉迷於 / 令人擔憂的

example.sentence: It is distressing that many teenagers are fixated on video games.

notes.text: 沉迷於 → 令人擔憂的
Super Reinforcement: are fixated on / hooked on / obsessed with / steeped in / preoccupied with / gripped by

phrases.zh: 過份重視 / 絕對不低估

example.sentence: Parents should by no means attach inordinate importance to their children's academic accomplishments.

notes.text: 過份重視 | 絕對不低估

example.sentence: The grade-fixated education system has exerted tremendous pressure on students.

notes.text: 施壓 | exert = put | tremendous = enormous / intolerable / unrelenting / undue (太多)
exert tremendous pressure = put a great strain on

example.sentence: The ever-rising youth suicide rate in the city speaks volumes about problems with our education system.

notes.text: 清楚說明（當e.g.）| problems with = defects in / faults in / imperfections in / shortcoming in

example.sentence: The exhibition captivates locals and tourists alike.

notes.text: 兩者都（不只A還是B）| 吸引
Pet owners and their pets often look alike (adj) = resemble

example.sentence: Going on a digital detox is a great way to rejuvenate our mind and body.

synonyms.word: embark on a social media cleanse

example.sentence: Travelling allows us to take a break from the hustle and bustle of city life.
There is a close correspondence between early childhood trauma and mental health issues in adulthood.

notes.text: 因果/密切關係 | ~密切有情

example.sentence: Hong Kong's high-stakes public examinations stifle innovation.
The serene village provides city dwellers with a much-needed getaway from their hectic schedules.

notes.text: 短假期/逃離 | 非常需要 | much needed = badly needed | serene = tranquil

example.sentence: Students will benefit considerably from the programme.

notes.text: 由…獲得重大好處 | × passive voice × be benefited from
2) The programme will benefit students (物) = be beneficial to / bring benefit to / be of benefit to

essays.title: Debate Speech: Abolishing Students' Class Position
essays.meta: 18 DSE P2 Q.5 | Mark: M1:21 / M2:21 / C:21 | Level: 5** | Debate Speech (Debating)

segments.content:
Good morning adjudicators, guests, the opposition side, and students,

I am Chris Wong, the captain of our team. Today, it is my
to stand here to argue for the motion that 'the policy of reporting students' class position should be abolished.'

marginNote.text: △的 -ve 中 | 最佳人個假借善意 / Topic Sen

segments.content: To all the guests and students sitting here in the hall, can you still vividly recall your feelings when you last received your report card with a long list of numbers indicating your position? Did you feel excited? Delighted?
? Or desperate? Are you satisfied with the fact that your academic results are determined by a set of numbers?
these numbers are good indicators that drive students to academic achievements, my team holds firmly to the belief that these rankings are
and the policy of reporting students' class position should be abolished. My team have three points to make.
Among all the demerits of this system, the most noteworthy one is that
segments.meaning: n. 缺點 | Topic Sentence structure

segments.content: the genuine purpose of education. Under the current education system in Hong Kong which
good grades, students attach inordinate importance to examinations as they believe that
would guarantee them a spot in university. This
the results of other classmates. If one is better than others, they will become
just to get a decent class position. If one loses this game, they might fall into
and deem themselves to be failures. The system would therefore merely
the meaning of education. With this in mind,

handNotes.text: e的 -ve 中 | 最佳人個假借善意 → Topic Sen

segments.content: On top of distorting the purpose of education, the system is unfair to students, especially the higher form students. As you all know, the NSS curriculum allows students to opt for different electives. As each subject may have a distinct mode of assessment, it makes no sense to compare students' class positions. I myself is a
example of this. As I am keen on

, I am studying English Literature and Chinese History as my electives. Yet, the scores I obtained were lower than classmates who take science subjects even if I was the best student in my elective class. Given that students take different electives, class position does not mean a lot and should thus be abolished.
Here comes our final argument — the system will
of 'Tiger Parents' in Hong Kong, ultimately putting a huge strain on students. Our city is infamous for

segments.meaning: phr. 虎媽與直升機父母/怪獸家長

segments.content: their children and desperately want them to excel in extra-curricular activities and exams alike. As parents may not know much about the school curriculum, the one and only indicator for them to
their children's performance would be their class position. The majority of students
parental expectations as a main source of pressure. These
the fact that disclosing class position to parents may make parents more demanding. It is lamentable to see that such
numbers on report cards ironically provoke bitter arguments between family members. Under this line of thinking, the system should be abolished.
Our dearest opponents would counter our stance, contending that class position forces students to improve their performance. Yet, such a claim is

. Isn't assigning students different grades already enough to reflect their strengths and shortcomings? Is it really necessary to impose an extra burden on students by disclosing their rankings? With this in mind, the opponent's argument does not stand.
To conclude, it is the system of reporting students' rankings that distorts the real meaning of education, that creates gross unfairness, and that triggers the
obvious that abolishing the system of reporting students' class position is the prerequisite for improving our problematic education system. Today's motion must therefore stand. Thank you!

essays.title: Letter to Parents: Digital Detox Workshop
essays.meta: Exam Language Focus R4.3-4.4 | Letter (Social Issues) | 電子排毒

segments.content:
Dear Parents,

I am writing to invite you and your child to take part in a two-day

segments.meaning: phr. 數碼健康/電子排毒
synonyms: embarking on a social media cleanse

segments.content: camp scheduled for next month. This is a wonderful opportunity for parents and students to put down electronic gadgets, spend quality time together and reconnect with each other.

segments.meaning: 在所有原因中最主要的是…

segments.content: why we are holding such a camp is that we would like to help parents and students
the online world. According to official figures, Hong Kong has one of the world's highest smartphone penetration rates at a
89.8% for people aged ten or above. Research has shown that there is
the use of social media and anxiety. The human
has also declined sharply since the first smartphones
of social media, we think it would be great if you could spend some
time with your child before their biggest challenge
in early March is a much-needed
it too. It is time we gave ourselves some spaces to slow down, unbreak our brains and
the relationship with our younger generation.

marginNote.text: 路途 | 高風格 | 非常需要 | 加強/鞏固(固)

segments.content: With the above reasons and goals in mind, I am glad to announce that the digital wellness camp will take place from 10 am on Saturday, March 9th, to 6pm on Sunday, March 10th, at the Wu Kwai Sha Youth Village. The fee for each family is only HK$900, covering meals, refreshments, workshop materials, a night's stay and all
charges. Each student will only be accompanied by one parent or
. After the camp, each student will need to make a short video sharing their experience. The best five clips will be posted on our school's website.

We believe that the camp will be a good time for students to interact with their classmates and parents outside the classroom. It is sincerely hoped that you will join with your child to participate in this meaningful activity. Please submit the reply slip

to the general office on or before February 15th. If you have any questions, please do not hesitate to contact me at 3345 6789.

Yours faithfully,
Chris Wong
6A Class Teacher
Hong Kong College
